context.description:
__Fishin' for Average Caucasian Boyfriends__ is a semi-autobiographical Game Boy game about expectations, queerness, and the possibility of dreaming bigger.

Based on a true conversation with my mom at a seafood restaurant, the game allows players to make the choice to fish–or not... 

What if we refuse to play the game?

<video autoplay muted controls>
  <source src="https://videos.ctfassets.net/t9p4c9x03bar/1Ixd1QjNBiqlmwuNIhv6ms/c99c02228311042e00a77f0fc0086c9f/playthrough-fish_cropped.mp4" type="video/mp4">
  Your browser does not support the video tag.
</video>

This game was originally developed as part of [FREEPLAY! A queer video games exhibit](https://www.kickstarter.com/projects/freeplay/freeplay) in Richmond, VA in January 2024. 

Built with GB Studio and playable for free on itch.io, the game was also adapted into a real, working Game Boy cartridge (complete with an instructions manual and holographic packaging) and sold as an [artist edition on Metalabel](https://jackieisonline.metalabel.com/fishin-for-average-caucasian-boyfriends) in summer of 2025.

![Fishing game cartridge in grass](//images.ctfassets.net/t9p4c9x03bar/75WfnNOfNiFSsGYcudq5iW/c530fb8f6a16a0d51c9b6a44b0d253a6/game_set_in_grass_square.jpg)

![fishing game box-back](//images.ctfassets.net/t9p4c9x03bar/2O7HI8PXs630aLppKONEHL/3dd84d8b5d1e1f509705a25d53dec6f2/box-back.jpg)

![fishing game manual in grass](//images.ctfassets.net/t9p4c9x03bar/21b9tlquzb4RNNXNvhzzfb/0e8cc739056d5c68ef38597d3cc2d834/manual_in_grass.jpg)

In January 2027, an edition of the piece was also donated into collections of the Strong National Museum of Play in Rochester, NY.

## Selected exhibitions
- __Art Games! Exhibition__, tiat gallery, March 2026, San Francisco
- __NEUROMANTICS__, Underground Art and Design x Danielle Paterson, December 2025, NYC
- __Player 2 Has Entered the Server__, Goethe Institute, June 2025, Bangkok
- __FREEPLAY! Queer Games Exhibition__, Iridian Gallery, January 2024, Richmond

## Acknowldgements

Special thanks to MeeNa Ko and Danielle Paterson for support at various stages of this project! <3
context.shortDescription: A short Game Boy game about expectations, queer identity, and the possibility of dreaming bigger.
context.title: Fishin' for Average Caucasian Boyfriends
